dimensions.note: Credential resolution stays delegated to the AWS SDK (fromIni/ambient) via the aws_credentials twin; no secrets vaulted or logged. The inline preflightSso wiring is replaced with the shared preflightSsoGate — same injectable token resolver, only the remediation message (no token bytes) surfaces. The known credentialModeField 'looks like a secret' WARN is the documented false positive (an enum of 'profile'/'env', not a secret).

The two pre-flight warn messages moved verbatim into the shared gate (asserted byte-for-byte by the canonical capturing-logger tests in _lib/aws_credentials_test.ts). The caller keeps its own 'vpc-inventory complete' info summary with VPC/error counts on the abort path. Structured placeholders throughout.

Policy unchanged, now owned by the gate: only expired aborts (one scan_error spreading the canonical sso fragment), a network blip warns and proceeds, empty ssoSession skips inside the gate. Per-(profile,region) failures still degrade to scan_error rows. Existing runScan pre-flight unit tests pass unchanged.

The runScan pre-flight paths remain pinned by the existing unit/smoke suites, which pass unchanged against the gate. The triplicated resolveBootstrapRegion tests were deleted from tests/ (the function moved to the canonical _lib); the full chain incl. whitespace-unset is covered once in _lib/aws_credentials_test.ts, which the repo gate runs.
Scan remains read-only and re-runnable with deterministic vpcKey/scanErrorKey keys. The gate is resolve-only; empty ssoSession preserves prior behavior exactly.

No AWS request/response mapping changed; SHARED_RETRY still spread into the EC2/STS clients; SDK pins unchanged at 3.1073.0. resolveBootstrapRegion moved verbatim to the twin (this package's copy was byte-identical to the canonical form), so bootstrap calls target the same region as before.
No new clients, handles, or files. The gate opens no SDK client of its own; existing cleanup paths untouched.

Manifest and model.version aligned at 2026.07.03.0. README unchanged (behavior identical). The entrypoint no longer exports resolveBootstrapRegion (now in the generated _lib twin); no README example referenced it. Test fixtures keep placeholder identifiers.

MECHANICAL Schema-Write Conformance re-verified at the changed call site: the abort-path writeError spreads the typed PreflightSsoErrorFragment over {profile:'', accountId:'', region:''} and appends scannedAt — all 8 ScanErrorSchema fields present, none extra, 'auth_expired' narrows into the kind enum. Schemas remain strict z.object, no passthrough.
vpc/scan_error keep lifetime 'infinite' and garbageCollection 10 — unchanged.
Read-only fan-out inventory, not a CRUD lifecycle model. Unchanged by this slice.
SSO pre-flight still opt-in and runs once before the per-profile loop; the deps.resolveSsoToken seam is preserved through the gate. requiredProfileSuffix refusal still fires before any AWS call.

MECHANICAL: key construction unchanged — scan_error keyed by canonical scanErrorKey(profile, region, service, phase); the preflight abort row keys identically to before. vpcKey unchanged and collision-free across (account, region, vpc).
writeResource-only producer; no readResource paths changed. The companion report's contract (vpc/scan_error rows) is byte-identical in shape.

model.version bumped 2026.06.26.1 -> 2026.07.03.0 with a matching no-op upgrade entry appended (identity upgradeAttributes; description notes the centralized pre-flight, no globalArguments schema changes). Chain tail equals model.version, verified by the colocated manifest-sync test.
